author: @davidfuller764
text: I appreciate you always. Not only that I do approve this message! Thanks

author: @donavanbangs
text: and if you are making small concrete parts that would be a great shaker to get rid of air bubbles also maybe for resin casts?

author: @michaelfisher3217
text: Thoughts on substituting ethyl alcohol for the isopropyl alcohol?  Here in Thailand, isopropyl alcohol is very expensive but we have loads of ethyl alcohol.

author: @richardfield7266
text: Have you thought about using one of those giant stand on vibrating exorcise plates? Now the fad has passed these are very cheap or free, if you are lucky with the added convenience of remote control. I used one of these for my big homemade garden riddle.

author: @ollieb9875
text: Guy up the road is moving house and selling off loads of gear, cheap apparently.. he's virtually giving away his welder and kit so I'm fixing to grab it.. we'll see 😀👍

text: oil leakage..?

author: @cohelixcohelix1212

author: @zaneaussie
text: lol what on earth are you up to now Rob :-D Geesus it's aaaliiivee lol Nice workl! Is there an easy way to make top down sonnicator/homogenizer? Similar to the one you have but without the price tag?

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: Now you are just showing off, lol - That is awesome mate, look forward to seeing it do the clean. I need one of those, I have a lot of metal screws and bolts to clean.

author: @endadalton
text: It always a tonic to see you laugh so much. Just what we all need at the end of a day. Thank You So Much.

author: @rexlarsen8287
text: What if you used velcro to attach to the tubs and plate

author: @paulmurgatroyd6372
text: A lot of sanders use velcro to hold the abrasive paper on. I wonder if there's some big, heavy duty velcro that could hold the containers on to the plate.

author: @farkookneebah
text: Hello again boss, please consider using a velcro sticker for attaching the boxes. It will allow you to change out the different size boxes as desired. Enjoy your day and carry on educating us :)

author: @darrellpidgeon6440
text: Awesome!  My first experience with vibration cleaning was an ultrasonic device for Rapid-o-graph pen nibs.  Since then I've kept vibrational cleansing ideas percolating in my mind.  One day I will experiment on getting air bubbles out of jars of stuff for canning.  I've also heard about stone polishing using the vibration method.  You are one of the best Makers I have ever seen.  Great work!!!

author: @BensWorkshop
text: Well, you've certainly gone big.

author: @hempev
text: Discovery always starts with "chuck our things in there and see what happens".

author: @chuxxsss
text: You make me laugh, on the words, "over the top". Oh Rob magic.

author: @FlyingFun.
text:
I had to read the description twice, first time I thought it said large vibrator cleaner lol.
Nice work again.

replies.author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
replies.text: You got a dirty mind. But that is funny like  when I was in a minuteman silo and remarked "mister that sure is one big erection you got there" I honestly had no idea why everyone was laughing so hard. A few almost fell over when I started asking questions about how much the American government pays for polished rockets. I think I was like 14 at the time. 30 years later people still laugh at that joke.

author: @mattjohnson7637
text: Your Dumpster diving skills and luck amaze me. Next time I see someone scrounging through a dumpster I won't assume that it is a homeless person. I will think "Hey maybe that's another super genius."

replies.author: @nedlyest
replies.text: You would be surprised by what people throw away. I scored a large propane smoker today.

text: GO BIG or go Home

text: Does that thing shake the whole building? :D

author: @chrisbingham3289
text: To fix your containers you could use Velcro strips,I was told the more you try to shake it the better it holds together. NASÀ uses it to hold the solar panels on their space station.

text: i get such a kick out of your enthusiasm

text: Wow, that huge sander is a real beast! I love it!

author: @AdricM
text: did you add an eccentric weight to the motor shaft?

author: @MisterRorschach90
text: Add a heating element to the vibrating plate. Heat increases the rate of rust removal. Imagine if you put a hot/stir plate on top. It would heat the solution and media to a certain temperature, and also stir it while the sander vibrates it. If you used special stir rods as the media it would both vibrate and stir the solution.

author: @victoryfirst2878
text: Hello Robert, I see you made a man size vibarator cleaner. Would you believe that I got a massive floor sander just like yours at a yard sale for just this purpose. Plan to make this happen this year. Nice work fella too. Peace.

author: @uncledodge9396
text: I'm wondering if a domestic dimmer switch would be man enough to use as a speed controller?  I built a tumbler for parts cleaning last year and in that I use sand, washing up liquid and Vinegar, works a treat! (made it out of a window winder motor).

author: @WayneTheSeine
text: Ha ha.....fire that puppy up. Robert lying in bed at night, "Hmmm......maybe an above ground pool......I could put my car in it and ....well....the possibilities are endless." :)  That is going to be one awesome parts cleaner indeed. Love it.
